mission.title: Install and Configure Cadence Workflow on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cadence is a distributed, scalable, durable, and highly available orchestration engine designed to execute asynchronous long-running business logic in a resilient manner. Installing Cadence allows you to manage complex workflows effectively and leverage its powerful features for your applications.

steps.title: Create a namespace for Cadence
steps.description:
Set up a dedicated namespace for Cadence to keep resources organized.
```bash
kubectl create namespace cadence
```

steps.title: Deploy Cadence backend components
steps.description:
Apply the Cadence deployment configurations to your cluster. Ensure you have the necessary configurations for your database (Cassandra/MySQL/PostgreSQL).
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cadence-workflow/cadence/main/deploy/kubernetes/cadence.yaml --namespace cadence
```

steps.title: Verify Cadence deployment
steps.description:
Check if all Cadence pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cadence
```

steps.title: Expose Cadence UI
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Cadence UI from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/cadence-frontend 8088:8088 --namespace cadence
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the deployment to set resource limits for the Cadence components.
```bash
kubectl edit deployment cadence-server --namespace cadence
# Add resource limits under spec.containers.resources:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"
```

uninstall.title: Remove Cadence deployment
uninstall.description:
Delete the Cadence resources from your cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cadence-workflow/cadence/main/deploy/kubernetes/cadence.yaml --namespace cadence
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the Cadence namespace to clean up all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cadence
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your Cadence data and configurations.
```bash
# Backup your database (e.g., Cassandra, MySQL, PostgreSQL) as per your database's backup procedure.
```

upgrade.title: Update deployment configuration
upgrade.description:
Fetch the latest Cadence deployment configuration.
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cadence-workflow/cadence/main/deploy/kubernetes/cadence.yaml --namespace cadence
```

upgrade.title: Verify upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Cadence pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cadence
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
This may indicate insufficient resources in your cluster. Check the resource requests and limits.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace cadence
```

troubleshooting.title: Cadence UI not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure that the port forwarding command is running and check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace cadence
```

troubleshooting.title: Database connection issues
troubleshooting.description:
Verify that your database is running and accessible from the Cadence pods.
```bash
kubectl logs <cadence-server-pod-name> --namespace cadence
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
Check the resource limits and consider increasing them if necessary.
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace cadence
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Cadence will have all pods running in the 'cadence' namespace, and you should be able to access the Cadence UI at http://localhost:8088. Additionally, workflows can be triggered and monitored through the UI.
codeSnippets:
kubectl edit deployment cadence-server --namespace cadence
# Add resource limits under spec.containers.resources:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
